behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is parser -> hook-handler handoff (not a single pure function), because it must both parse and then "proceed through normal hook input handling." REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process wiring is sufficient. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real process/stdin. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: Deterministic plumbing proof: "Valid JSON payloads ... parse successfully". Model-output exclusion proof: behavior only references JSON parsing and hook handling, with no AI/ML decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process test wiring the real parser and hook input handler. Feed valid JSON where fields contain code fences, backticks, and `$vars`; assert parse success, no parse-failure trace, and continuation into the normal handled path.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this checks interaction between parser error output and trace-record construction/sink contract. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text. Rejected System because "Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record" can be validated in-process. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: Deterministic plumbing proof: "malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record". Model-output exclusion proof: expected output is a fixed trace signal/context, not model-generated content.
behaviors.test_description: Use real parser + trace emission modules in-process. Submit malformed JSON (without synthetic trailing-junk shortcuts) and assert a `json_parse_failed` record is emitted and includes diagnostic context pinpointing malformed segment.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior targets one local policy mapping path. Text explicitly says "local error-policy path," indicating a single object/function boundary. REAL-INFRA: none required. Rejected Integration because no required module handoff is stated beyond local mapping. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: Deterministic plumbing proof: "maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent." Model-output exclusion proof: this is policy/state mapping logic, with no model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the error-policy function with a parse-failure input object and assert: returns handled/no-throw result and telemetry intent flag is set exactly as expected.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires "real process execution" and malformed "stdin" plus exit behavior, which depends on subprocess/OS pipe semantics. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can miss crash/exit-order issues. Rejected Integration due explicit real-process requirement. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: Deterministic plumbing proof: "malformed stdin does not crash the hook process ... emits the expected trace signal before exit." Model-output exclusion proof: scope is process robustness and trace emission, not AI/ML output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the real hook process as a subprocess, write malformed JSON to stdin, close stdin, and assert: process exits without crash, and expected parse-failure trace signal is emitted before process termination.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior names a "real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)"; stdin/process wiring is part of failure boundary. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing stdin/process can hide invocation-path defects. Rejected Integration because concrete text requires real invocation path. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: Deterministic plumbing proof: "stdin -> parser -> trace sink" and "expected trace artifact". Model-output exclusion proof: no model classification/generation quality is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Execute hook through its real entrypoint with malformed stdin and validate end artifact from the actual trace sink (file/log channel configured in test env) matches expected parse-failure signal/content.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: regression requires comparing outcomes across parser + handling + trace classification behavior, not just isolated parsing helper logic. REAL-INFRA: no explicit real subprocess/OS requirement in this behavior text. Rejected System because distinction can be proven with in-process module wiring. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: Deterministic plumbing proof: "distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads". Model-output exclusion proof: distinction is based on JSON validity and trace classification, not AI output content.
behaviors.test_description: Create paired in-process regression cases through parser/handler/trace modules: (a) valid JSON containing markdown-heavy strings, (b) truly malformed JSON. Assert only (b) emits parse-failure signal and (a) follows normal path, preventing markdown false positives.
